title: erythroxylum cincinnatum
intro: Erythroxylum cincinnatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Erythroxylaceae, commonly known as the coca shrub or Coca of the Amazon. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, where its medicinal properties include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects, which contribute to its various health benefits such as wound healing and pain relief. The plant's leaves and bark are often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties. However, due to its potential psychoactive effects, Erythroxylum cincinnatum is regulated in many countries and its use is subject to strict controls.
